mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling consumer demand and corporate earnings declines.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, driving robust corporate revenue growth and yielding positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflect capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks, prompting immediate market repricing of near-term earnings and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in more than 50% of industrial production declines occurring after five years of continuous GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 4.0% signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and corporate financing costs, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of periods where the rate threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which systematically boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive equity market performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and declining retail sales indicates weakening aggregate demand that immediately erodes consumer-facing equity valuations, producing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences or fails to maintain statistical significance across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Improvements in the index of industrial production during post-recession recovery phases signal expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of recovery-phase industrial production increases, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample validation.
